meaning: n.装饰船头的雕像,破浪神,有名无实的领袖
examples: "At 81, he could only be a figurehead or 'caretaker' director-general," said diplomats, who also point to a rigorous Egyptian campaign against him.
At least at the start, he will be little more than a figurehead.
The media demanded to know whether Reagan was in charge or was just a figurehead.
In recent years, Mr. Paterno had largely become a figurehead as Penn State's coach.
The British monarch has no political power but Elizabeth has immense power as a figurehead.
